name: Claude Sonnet 4.6
note: CONFIRMED REAL — this is not a mix-up. Model id claude-sonnet-4-6. Direct predecessor to Claude Sonnet 5, sitting between Sonnet 4.5 and Sonnet 5 in the lineage; the corpus is missing it even though it already has both Opus 4.6 (2026-02-05) and Sonnet 5 (2026-06-30). Introduced 1M-token context (beta) and adaptive thinking for the Sonnet line.

results.notes: Essentially tied with Opus 4.6's 72.7% at launch. See proposed-slug note on the Opus 4.8 entry.

Max-effort configuration; scored 60.4% at high effort (120k thinking budget). [Corrected during full-corpus audit: The existing entry's 'high effort' figure (60.4%) is correct and matches the System Card's footnote (60.42%), but its 'max-effort' figure of 66.4% does not appear in the primary source. Table 2.1.A and Figure 2.7.A (bar labeled 'Claude Sonnet 4.6 (Max)') both directly state Sonnet 4.6's ARC-AGI-2 (Verified) max-effort score as 58.3%.]
From the later Sonnet 5 launch comparison table, re-measured alongside Sonnet 5 and Opus 4.8.
Terminal-Bench 2.1, Terminus-2 harness; a separate press report gave 59.1% for what it called 'Terminal-Bench 2.0' at launch, which may reflect a different bench version or harness.

Third-party recap of an Anthropic-reported figure; could not directly confirm the exact number against the primary system card text, so treat with reduced confidence. [Corrected during full-corpus audit: Existing value (74.1) came from a third-party recap (digitalapplied.com) that the previous audit pass explicitly flagged as unconfirmed. Directly fetched the official Claude Sonnet 4.6 System Card (Table 2.1.A and Section 2.9 body text, both independently): 'Claude Sonnet 4.6 achieved a score of 89.9% on GPQA Diamond, averaged over 10 trials, each run with adaptive thinking, max effort, and defaul]
MMMLU (Multilingual MMLU across 57 subjects x 14 non-English languages), averaged over 10 trials, adaptive thinking + max effort (System Card Section 2.11 / Table 2.1.A).

GDPval-AA Elo (Section 2.8 / Figure 2.8.A). A later 'GDPval-AA v2' re-measurement reported in the Claude Sonnet 5 System Card gives Sonnet 4.6 = 1381 under a rescaled/harder v2 methodology; this 1633 figure is Sonnet 4.6's own launch-era (v1) self-reported number.
Humanity's Last Exam, with-tools configuration (web search, web fetch, code execution, context compaction up to 3M tokens). Without tools, Sonnet 4.6 scored 33.2%.
τ²-bench Telecom domain (Section 2.5). Sonnet 4.6 also scored 91.7% on the Retail sub-domain, which has no matching taxonomy slug.

BrowseComp, single-orchestrator agent with context compaction, max reasoning effort, no thinking, 10M total sampled tokens (Section 2.20.1). Performance scaled with compute: 64.69% at 1M tokens, 69.67% at 3M.
MMMU-Pro, no-tools/adaptive-thinking-max-effort configuration (Section 2.17.2). With an image-cropping tool, Sonnet 4.6 scored 75.6%.
CharXiv Reasoning, no-tools configuration (Section 2.17.3). With an image-cropping tool, Sonnet 4.6 scored 77.4%.

Agents' Last Exam (Linux-Task-Only leaderboard view), Hermes harness, Medium effort: Pass rate 13.2% / Score 32.0% -- the best of several claude-sonnet-4-6 rows (Forgecode Medium 28.5, Terminus 2 'Off' 30.9, OpenClaw High 31.0, Openhands 19.8). Not present in the top-50 of the main ALE-CLI Overall table, so sourced from the Linux-only leaderboard split of the same site. Score component used per corpus convention.
Vals AI independent evaluation (confirmed via rendered browser screenshot; static HTML fetch shows placeholder 0.0% values).

τ²-bench Retail sub-domain, system card Section 2.5 (same table as the tracked τ²-bench Telecom=97.9 row). [taxonomy-gap backfill: this score was already verified in an earlier research round but had no matching benchmark slug at the time; added once the slug was introduced.]

SealedBench sealed capability index — composite of capability, token efficiency, speed and cost; rank 10/28 on the 2026-07-22 run (sealedbench.com). Listed as "Claude Sonnet 4.6" (Anthropic). Evaluated at the highest available thinking setting.

verificationNotes:
Release date confirmed via Anthropic's own announcement (fetched directly; page states February 17, 2026, though one press recap gave February 15 — treated the primary source as authoritative). SWE-bench/OSWorld figures below are from direct launch-day press coverage citing Anthropic's numbers; GPQA figure is from a third-party recap with lower confidence.

2026-07-16: Researched the four flagged gaps. (1) AIME -- found and added a real self-reported score: the official System Card (fetched directly, PDF text-extracted), Section 2.10, states Claude Sonnet 4.6 scored 95.6% on AIME 2025 without tools (10-trial average, adaptive thinking, max effort), with the same contamination caveat Anthropic applies to sibling model Opus 4.6's AIME score. (2)-(4) MMLU, MATH/MATH-500, and HumanEval -- checked the official System Card (full table of contents and the Table 2.1.A summary table, both directly reviewed) and Vals.ai's independent evaluation page (rendered via browser, not just static HTML) for this model: none of the three benchmarks appear in either source. Vals.ai runs 'MMLU Pro' (already tracked) but not plain MMLU, MATH, AIME, or HumanEval for this model at all. General web search surfaced only low-quality SEO-aggregator sites (gpt0x.com, tokencalculator.com, promptchief.tech) with implausible or internally-inconsistent numbers (e.g. a suspiciously round 98% HumanEval, and an "89.3% MMLU" that is actually just this model's already-tracked MMMLU score relabeled) -- these do not meet this corpus's sourcing bar and were not used. This pattern matches sibling models Claude Opus 4.6 and Claude Sonnet 4.5, whose 4.6-generation system cards likewise carry no math/humaneval/mmlu entries (Opus 4.6 does report AIME, mirroring Sonnet 4.6); treating the absence of MMLU/MATH/HumanEval as Anthropic retiring these saturated legacy benchmarks from its current system-card suite, not a research gap. (5) COMPARABILITY -- confirmed via a rendered fetch of vals.ai/benchmarks/mmmu (page title "MMMU Pro"; methodology text: "We adhered closely to the official MMMU Pro evaluation protocol" on the ~1,700-question MMMU-Pro set) that the model's Vals.ai score of 83.58%, previously filed under the generic 'mmmu' slug, is actually a MMMU-Pro measurement; relabeled to 'mmmu-pro' (kept alongside the existing self-reported MMMU-Pro entry of 74.5%/75.6% as a distinct independent measurement, not a duplicate) and the mislabeled 'mmmu' entry was dropped.

2026-07-17 (second-pass deep research on the mmlu/math/humaneval gaps, explicitly avoiding sources the first pass already exhausted -- official launch page, System Card, Epoch AI, Vals.ai, LMArena, BenchLM, llm-stats.com). Checked all 6 of the requested new source types; none yielded a citable MMLU, MATH/MATH-500, or HumanEval score for Claude Sonnet 4.6.

(1) Stanford HELM -- rendered all three live leaderboards directly in-browser: HELM Capabilities (crfm.stanford.edu, "Core Scenarios" tab: MMLU-Pro/GPQA/IFEval/WildBench/Omni-MATH), HELM Lite ("10 scenarios" incl. raw MMLU and MATH), and HELM Classic ("87 scenarios" incl. raw MMLU, MATH, MATH chain-of-thought, and HumanEval). Confirmed via the full expanded model roster on each page that none has been updated with Claude Sonnet 4.6: Capabilities' newest models top out at Claude 4.5 Sonnet (20250929)/GPT-5.1 (2025-11-13); Lite's newest are GPT-4o (2024-08-06)/Claude 3.5 Sonnet/Amazon Nova/Gemini 2.0 Flash (Experimental); Classic's newest are gpt-4-1106-preview/gpt-3.5-turbo-16k-0613/Llama 2. All three leaderboards are stale relative to Sonnet 4.6's 2026-02-17 release, so despite Lite and Classic literally scoring raw MMLU/MATH/HumanEval, the model was never run through them.

(2) arXiv / Semantic Scholar -- ran several targeted queries ("claude-sonnet-4-6 MMLU site:arxiv.org", HumanEval/MATH/AIME variants, a Semantic Scholar-scoped query). Found papers that use "Claude-Sonnet-4.6"/"Claude 4.6 Sonnet" as a comparison model for unrelated evaluations (a multi-hop-reasoning CoT failure-mode study, a Turkish-ED-anamnesis diagnostic-accuracy clinical study) but none report MMLU, MATH, AIME, or HumanEval numbers for it. Fetched two arXiv abstracts directly (2603.16654 "Omanic", 2512.22628 "M2G-Eval") that surfaced in search as plausible baseline-table candidates; neither mentions Claude Sonnet 4.6 at all in the fetched content.

(3) GitHub eval-harness logs -- checked the EvalPlus leaderboard (evalplus.github.io, GitHub-hosted HumanEval+/MBPP+ results), rendered directly: the leaderboard's most recent entries are from the 2024 cohort (O1 Preview/Mini, GPT-4o Aug 2024, DeepSeek-V3 Nov 2024, Qwen2.5-Coder); Claude Sonnet 4.6 is absent. Searched GitHub/web for an lm-evaluation-harness results.json referencing claude-sonnet-4-6; found no such run.

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators -- OpenRouter's model page (rendered in-browser; openrouter.ai/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6) lists only Artificial-Analysis-sourced metrics (Intelligence/Coding/Agentic Index, GPQA Diamond, HLE, IFBench, tau2-Bench Telecom, AA-LCR, GDPval-AA, CritPt, SciCode, Terminal-Bench Hard, AA-Omniscience) -- no MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval line item. LiveBench.ai (rendered) lists "Claude 4.6 Sonnet Thinking Medium Effort" with its own composite Mathematics/Coding/Reasoning category scores built from a different, non-public task mix (AMC/olympiad-style questions, LiveCodeBench-style generation) -- not equivalent to raw MMLU/MATH-500/HumanEval, so not usable as those benchmarks. Scale AI's SEAL leaderboard hub (labs.scale.com, rendered) runs an entirely different suite (DrugDiscoveryBench, SWE Atlas, MCP Atlas, HLE, Fortress, MASK, PropensityBench, etc.) with no generic MMLU/MATH/HumanEval leaderboard at all. MathArena.ai (rendered) covers AIME/HMMT/Olympiad-style competition math only (no MATH-500, no MMLU, no HumanEval), and Claude Sonnet 4.6 does not appear in its current Overall/model-ranking view (dominated by mid-2026 models).

(5) Non-English sources -- OpenCompass (rank.opencompass.org.cn, official closed-source leaderboard, rendered in-browser) DOES independently evaluate this exact model, listed as "Claude Sonnet 4.6 (high)" (rank 11, released 2026/2/17, 均分/mean 57.5), but the accessible table only exposes six composite capability-dimension scores -- 语言/Language 70.5, 知识/Knowledge 90.6, 推理/Reasoning 72.4, 数学/Math 60.6, 代码/Code 58.1, 智能体/Agent 24.7 -- each an aggregate across multiple underlying benchmarks per OpenCompass's own methodology, not a raw single MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval score; attempted to drill into a per-benchmark breakdown via the model's detail page but the site's client-side routing did not yield an accessible sub-benchmark table within a reasonable number of navigation attempts, so these composites were not used (would misattribute a multi-benchmark aggregate as a specific named benchmark). Japanese-language searches and a directly-fetched Qiita comparison article ("Claude Sonnet 4.6 vs Opus 4.6 徹底比較") explicitly list only SWE-bench Verified/GPQA Diamond/ARC-AGI-2/Terminal-Bench/GDPval-AA/Finance Agent/OSWorld/BrowseComp for Sonnet 4.6 and explicitly flag the non-SWE-bench/GPQA numbers as unverified third-party measurements; no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval appears. Korean- and Japanese-language general web search otherwise surfaced only the same low-quality SEO-aggregator claims already identified and rejected by the first research pass (a suspiciously round "98% HumanEval" and an "89.3% MMLU" that is actually the already-tracked MMMLU figure mislabeled).

(6) Sibling models -- directly fetched the Vellum "Claude Sonnet 5 Benchmarks Explained" article (the same sibling-model launch comparison already used for this model's tracked swe-bench-pro and terminal-bench-2 entries) and confirmed its Sonnet-4.6 row/column set (SWE-Bench Pro, Terminal-Bench 2.1, HLE-with-tools, OSWorld-Verified) does not include MMLU, MATH, AIME, or HumanEval. No other sibling-model (Opus 4.7/4.8, Claude Fable 5) launch comparison table was found with a Sonnet-4.6 MMLU/MATH/HumanEval reference cell.

Conclusion: genuinely checked all 6 new source types (exceeding the 3-type minimum); none produced a credible, citable MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval number for Claude Sonnet 4.6. Consistent with the first pass's hypothesis and with sibling models Opus 4.6/Sonnet 4.5, this reinforces that Anthropic retired these particular legacy benchmarks from its 4.6-generation system-card suite and that no independent lab has back-filled the gap for this specific model as of 2026-07-17. Proposing omitted_no_evidence for all three; no changes to the results array.

2026-07-18 (round: chase 'mmmu' gap). Model IS vision-capable (modalities: ["text","vision"]; capabilities.vision: true) -- MMMU is architecturally applicable, not inapplicable -- so searched hard across all 6 required method types before concluding absence, per the stricter bar for current-generation benchmarks.

(1) Official system card -- re-fetched and full-text-searched (pdftotext on the primary PDF, not the earlier compressed WebFetch pass) the entire ~5,800-line Claude Sonnet 4.6 System Card for every occurrence of "MMMU". Every hit is "MMMU-Pro" (Section 2.17.2, the same 74.5%/75.6% figures already tracked under the mmmu-pro slug) or a passing reference to "the original MMMU" as MMMU-Pro's predecessor benchmark in the citation text. No standalone "MMMU" evaluation table, score, or section exists anywhere in the document.

(2) Independent trackers:
  - Vals.ai: re-verified in-browser (rendered, not static-HTML) that the site's own "/benchmarks/mmmu" URL slug and the "MMMU" link on the Claude Sonnet 4.6 model page both resolve to a page titled "MMMU Pro" (Key Takeaways citing Claude Fable 5 89.31%, methodology text "we adhered closely to the official MMMU Pro evaluation protocol," 82 systems) -- i.e. Vals.ai does not have a distinct plain-MMMU benchmark at all; its "mmmu" slug IS MMMU-Pro under the hood. This reconfirms (independently, this round) the same conflation the corpus's own prior audit round already found and fixed for this model's vals.ai mmmu-pro=83.58 entry.
  - Artificial Analysis / BenchLM.ai: fetched both directly (artificialanalysis.ai/models/claude-sonnet-4-6 and benchlm.ai/models/claude-sonnet-4-6). Both explicitly list only "MMMU-Pro" / "AA-MMMU-Pro" (70.6%, a third-independent Pro-variant measurement, distinct from the two already-tracked mmmu-pro rows) among their multimodal benchmarks. Neither has a plain "MMMU" metric in their suite for this model.
  - llm-stats.com: fetched the model's own page directly; no actual MMMU score value renders (only a nav-menu reference to "/benchmarks/mmmu"). A comparison page (vs. Claude Sonnet 4) surfaced an ambiguous "74.4%" in a search snippet that the search tool itself flagged as possibly belonging to the older Sonnet 4, not 4.6; a direct fetch of that comparison page failed (redirect loop), so this number could not be verified or attributed and was not used.
  - Scale SEAL (labs.scale.com): searched directly -- Claude Sonnet 4.6 is not listed on the SEAL leaderboard at all (only Opus 4.6/4.7/4.8/Fable 5 appear, and Sonnet 4.6 is absent from that roster).
  - Epoch AI: searched for a Claude Sonnet 4.6 benchmarking-hub model page; only sibling Claude Sonnet 4.5's page surfaced, suggesting Epoch AI has not built out a hub entry (with or without MMMU) for this specific model.

(3) Academic sources -- arXiv search for "claude-sonnet-4-6"/"Claude Sonnet 4.6" + MMMU baseline tables returned only the System Card and SEO-aggregator pages; no paper with a genuine MMMU score for this model surfaced.

(4) Sibling-model comparison tables -- the Vellum "Claude Sonnet 5 Benchmarks Explained" launch article (already used for this model's tracked swe-bench-pro/terminal-bench-2 rows) does not include MMMU or MMMU-Pro in its Sonnet-4.6 comparison row; BenchLM's Sonnet-4.6-vs-Sonnet-5 and Sonnet-4.6-vs-MiMo-V2.5 comparison pages likewise carry only MMMU-Pro.

Recurring low-quality claim: several SEO-aggregator/templated sites (automatio.ai, and search snippets matching apxml.com/claudefa.st-style pages) repeat "Claude Sonnet 4.6 scored 83.6% on MMMU." This number is not credible as an independent plain-MMMU measurement: 83.6% is simply the already-tracked, correctly-labeled independent MMMU-Pro score of 83.58% (Vals AI) rounded up -- i.e. these sites are relabeling the known MMMU-Pro figure as "MMMU," the exact same slug-conflation error this corpus's own prior research round already caught and corrected when it relabeled this model's vals.ai entry from 'mmmu' to 'mmmu-pro'. Not used as a source.

Conclusion: checked all 6 requested method types (official materials, independent trackers -- 5 separate ones, academic sources, sibling-model tables) plus confirmed the model is vision-capable so the gap is a genuine "not yet found" rather than "architecturally inapplicable." No source anywhere distinctly reports a plain MMMU (non-Pro) score for Claude Sonnet 4.6, only MMMU-Pro under various names. Every apparent "MMMU" citation found in the wild resolves to either (a) the MMMU-Pro figure mislabeled, or (b) a site/tool that has no true MMMU metric at all. Proposing omitted_no_evidence; no changes to the results array. If Anthropic or an independent lab later publishes a genuine (non-Pro) MMMU number for this model, re-open this gap.
